findings.issue: Task 3 (Wave 1) lists `.nvmrc` as a file to read, but `.nvmrc` is created by Task 1 (also Wave 1). In a strict parallel execution model, Task 3 may start before Task 1 has written `.nvmrc`, creating a read-before-create race condition. Task 3 does embed the expected content (Node 24) in its own Details section, making the read advisory rather than load-bearing, but the dependency is undeclared.
findings.fix: Add `depends_on: [1]` to Task 3, or explicitly note in Task 3 that the `.nvmrc` content is advisory (Node 24) and does not block execution if the file is not yet present. The cleaner fix is `depends_on: [1]`, which also ensures `.nvmrc` exists before `nvm use` or `pnpm install` is run.

findings.issue: Task 6 (Gitleaks hook) is placed in Wave 2 with `depends_on: [1]`. Since it only requires the `scripts/` directory (created by Task 1, Wave 1), Task 6 could be placed in Wave 1 to maximize parallelism. Wave 2 placement is not incorrect — it will execute correctly — but it delays Task 6 behind the Wave 1 batch unnecessarily.
findings.fix: Consider moving Task 6 to Wave 1 (remove from Wave 2). Requires no change to depends_on — `depends_on: [1]` would remain valid if Task 6 is assigned wave 1 only after Task 1 completes. Alternatively, leave as-is since the delay is one wave and correctness is not affected.

findings.issue: Task 7 uses `documentation-writing` skill with `code-reviewer` as the sole reviewer. The standard skill-reviewer mapping does not define a dedicated `documentation-reviewer`, and `code-reviewer` reviewing Markdown documentation is not a natural fit — the code-reviewer skill focuses on source code quality gates (TDD, coupling, patterns), not doc accuracy or completeness. The reviewer is not wrong, but the match is loose.
findings.fix: This is acceptable given the skill catalog has no `documentation-reviewer`. No change required — note as acknowledged mismatch. If a documentation-reviewing agent is added to the catalog in future, Task 7 should be updated.
